article_title: Blue Origin Targets April 12 for New Glenn's First Booster Reuse, Carrying the Largest Commercial Satellite Ever to Orbit

content_preview.title: Blue Origin Targets April 12 for New Glenn's First Booster Reuse, Carrying the Largest Commercial Satellite Ever to Orbit
content_preview.summary: New Glenn-3 will refly the booster that landed after delivering NASA's ESCAPADE Mars probes, deploying AST SpaceMobile's 2,400-square-foot BlueBird 7 direct-to-device satellite.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Blue Origin is targeting no earlier than April 12, 2026, for the launch of New Glenn-3 (NG-3), a mission that will attempt to refly an orbital-class booster for the first time in the company's history while delivering the largest commercial communications satellite ever deployed in low Earth orbit, according to [NextBigFuture](https://www.nextbigfuture.com/2026/04/blue-origin-new-glenn-targets-april-launch-of-ast-space-mobile-satellite.html).
